title: heterotheca villosa var. pedunculata
intro: Heterotheca villosa var. pedunculata, also known as hairy false goldeneye, is a plant species in the sunflower family that is native to the central and eastern regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The hairy false goldeneye has been reported to offer various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and soothing digestive problems, due to its rich content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Heterotheca villosa var. pedunculata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for topical or internal use, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
